{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Normalization and the Residual Stream {#sec-normalization-residual}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- The connective tissue that makes deep transformers trainable: residual
     connections and normalization. The "residual stream" as a mental model. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Residual connections

<!-- Blocks read from and write to a shared residual stream; gradients flow;
     the additive, incremental view of computation. -->

## Normalization

<!-- LayerNorm vs. RMSNorm; what statistic is removed and why it stabilizes
     optimization. -->

## Pre-norm vs. post-norm

<!-- Placement of the norm relative to the residual; why pre-norm won for
     deep, stable training. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
